title: Charity regulator appoints Interim Managers to College Farm Trust
body: "The charity was placed under statutory inquiry in August 2024 to investigate a range of concerns about trustee decision making.    The Commission has now appointed Interim Managers to the charity, whose role is to undertake certain duties in line with the charity’s governing document.    The Interim Managers were appointed on 19 December 2024 and will be in control of the charity to undertake the duties specified in the appointment order.    Frances Coulson and Robert Paterson of Wedlake Bell LLP are tasked with, including but not limited to:     securing the charity’s property and assets    recruiting members    calling an AGM at which trustees will be elected    The Commission’s inquiry into the charity remains ongoing.    ENDS   Notes to editors       The Charity Commission is the independent, non-ministerial government department that registers and regulates charities in England and Wales. Its ambition is to be an expert regulator that is fair, balanced, and independent so that charity can thrive. This ambition will help to create and sustain an environment where charities further build public trust and ultimately fulfil their essential role in enhancing lives and strengthening society    Information about the Charity Commission’s appointment of Interim Managers can be found on GOV.UK   More information on the scope of the inquiry into College Farm Trust can be found in the announcement   Public information about College Farm Trust is available on the Register of Charities: COLLEGE FARM TRUST - 1074936     Press office     Email pressenquiries@charitycommission.gov.uk     Out of hours press office contact number:  07785 748787      "
